title: stachys citrina
intro: Stachys citrina, also known as Lemon Catmint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Stachys citrina has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and acting as an expectorant to relieve coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
